label: Law Firms

seo.title: Law Firm Funding for Case Loads, Payroll, and Expansion
seo.description: Capital options for law firms that need working capital, hiring runway, marketing spend, or expansion funding.

hero.headline: Law Firm Funding for Case Loads, Payroll, and Expansion
hero.subheadline: Law firms need capital to hire, market, and handle uneven case cashflow without slowing the practice down.

problem: Case work pays over time, not always when expenses hit.
Hiring and marketing need cash before revenue catches up.
Generic financing pages do not respect the firm's operating cadence.
solution: Position funding around case-load cycles, operating runway, and growth planning so the firm can move without feeling rushed into the wrong product.

proof.title: Professional-services framing
proof.description: The page is written for a law firm's business realities, not consumer lending language.

proof.title: Simple next action
proof.description: A booking CTA is easier to trust than a complicated form.

proof.title: Clear buyer fit
proof.description: The language helps the right firms self-select fast.

howItWorks: Share your firm type, case mix, and capital goal.
Compare options based on timing, structure, and repayment pressure.
Book the path that fits your operating model.

useCases.title: Hiring runway
useCases.description: Use it when you need staff before the next wave of cases closes.

useCases.title: Marketing spend
useCases.description: Use it to fuel ads, intake, or referral growth campaigns.

useCases.title: Expansion capital
useCases.description: Use it when a new office or practice area needs upfront investment.

objections.q: Does this work for contingency-heavy firms?
objections.a: Yes. That is exactly the type of cashflow story this page should support.

objections.q: What if I only need short-term working capital?
objections.a: Then the page should surface the fastest lane and keep the conversation simple.
It should not. The goal is clarity, not pressure.

finalCta.label: Book a Firm Review
finalCta.description: Use this page to connect case-flow timing with a real capital path.

brief.audience: law firms operators who need working capital, growth capital, or project funding
